_about: L2b+L5b diagnostic subset of the reasoning ladder — the two traps the general grid failed. For the code-specialization run.

items.question: What was net revenue (sales revenue minus returned amounts) overall?
items.notes: Combine two tables and SUBTRACT. A model that joins sales->returns row-wise DOUBLE-COUNTS revenue (some sales have >1 return) and drops non-returned sales — a wrong number that still runs. Classic fan-out trap.

items.question: For each product, what fraction of its revenue was returned? (returned amount divided by that product's sales revenue). Rank products from highest return-fraction to lowest.
items.notes: Hardest: returns reach product only THROUGH sales (returns has sale_id, not product) — join returns->sales, aggregate returns per product, aggregate sales per product SEPARATELY (or double-count via the join), LEFT JOIN so zero-return products survive, then divide. Many independent ways to go wrong.
